channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Automated Eosinophil Quantification Using Deep Learning to Predict Therapy Escalation in Pediatric Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Clinical and translational gastroenterology - Emerging evidence implicates eosinophils as important modulators of disease activity and therapeutic response in ulcerative colitis. Automated image analysis provides a scalable and reproducible ap...

item.title: Machine Learning Analysis of Serum Metabolomics for Multi-Disease Classification and Proteome-Metabolome Pathway Insights in T2D, IBD, and CRC.
item.description: Journal of proteome research - Type 2 diabetes (T2D), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and colorectal cancer (CRC) share overlapping metabolic alterations that hinder early, disease-specific diagnosis. Using publicly available ...

item.title: Development and validation of a predictive model for early failure of biological therapy in perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease based on three-dimensional transrectal ultrasound.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Despite the clinical use of three-dimensional transrectal ultrasound (3D-TRUS) in pfCD, no validated model exists to predict early biologic treatment failure. Our objective was to develop and valid...

item.title: Advancing IBD Management: A Literature Review on the Role of Non-Invasive Blood-Based Biomarkers in Predicting and Assessing Pharmacodynamic Response to Treatment.
item.description: Clinical and translational science - Review - Reliable biomarkers that enable noninvasive, longitudinal assessment of disease activity and therapeutic response remain a major unmet need in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). While colonic biopsi...

item.title: Spatial transcriptomics atlas of inflammatory bowel disease to guide implementation in research consortiums and clinical trials.
item.description: Nature communications - Using over 100 intestinal tissue sections from non-diseased controls and patients with ulcerative colitis or Crohn's disease across multiple inflammatory bowel disease consortia, we construct a spa...

item.title: Development and validation of a novel latex particle-enhanced turbidimetric immunoassay for fecal calprotectin.
item.description: Biotechnology letters - Fecal calprotectin (FCP) is a crucial non-invasive biomarker for diagnosing and monitoring inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, the standard enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) is time-...

item.title: Discovery of Benzofuran-2-Carboxylic Acid Derivatives as Potent PTPRO Inhibitors with Oral Efficacy for Treatment of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Journal of medicinal chemistry - Emerging evidence implicates PTPRO as a pivotal regulator in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Screening a benzofuran-2-carboxylic acid-based library identified a lead PTPRO inhibitor. Structure-gu...

item.title: EB1010, a putative Christensenellaceae-derived microbial peptide, exhibits anti-inflammatory activity and therapeutic potential in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Gut microbes - The gut microbiota plays a pivotal role in modulating the immune system, fostering immune tolerance, and defending against pathogens. Among its bioactive products, microbiota-derived peptides hold ...

item.title: Lyophilized mesenchymal stromal cell-derived extracellular vesicles for the oral treatment of inflammatory bowel disease: a novel cell-free therapeutic strategy.
item.description: Stem cell research & therapy - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD), is a chronic and relapsing inflammatory disorder of the gastrointestinal tract. Current standard tre...

item.title: Red Cell Distribution Width as an Independent Marker of Objective Disease Activity in Ulcerative Colitis and Crohn's Disease: A Large Real-World Cohort of 1000 Patients.
item.description: The Turkish journal of gastroenterology : the official journal of Turkish Society of Gastroenterology - The treat-to-target (T2T) strategy requires objective biomarkers in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Red cell distribution width (RDW) is of interest and routinely available; however, its incremen...

item.title: Epidemiological Trends in Inflammatory Bowel Disease in Korea: Age-Related Shifts and Urban-Rural Disparities in a Nationwide Study, 2004-2015.
item.description: Yonsei medical journal - The incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is rapidly increasing in newly industrialized Asian countries. However, nationwide epidemiological shifts, especially age-specific trends and urban...

item.title: Comparative Outcomes After Index Myocardial Infarction in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Journal of clinical gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic systemic inflammatory disorder associated with elevated cardiovascular risk. While prior studies have linked IBD to increased risk of myocardial infarc...

item.title: Socioeconomic disparities in biologic persistence among patients with inflammatory bowel disease are associated with increased health care utilization.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Biologic persistence, defined as the total uninterrupted time on a biologic agent, is associated with improved outcomes in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, social determinants of health (...

item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease and primary sclerosing cholangitis: immunological links and clinical outlook.
item.description: Expert review of clinical immunology - Review - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) represents a progressive fibroinflammatory cholangiopathy intricately linked to inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), forming a distinctive overlap syndrome driven ...

item.title: Mechanisms of inflammatory bowel diseases: Insights from human genetics and chemical biology.
item.description: Immunity - Review - Human genetics has proven remarkably productive in linking genetic variants to a wide variety of diseases. Discovery initiatives from medical and population genetics have been especially prolific i...

item.title: Exploring alterations in the gut resistome in medically treated inflammatory bowel disease patients.
item.description: BMC microbiology -

item.title: Factors Associated With Lack of Pre-Operative Stoma Site Marking Before Ostomy Surgery.
item.description: The Journal of surgical research - Preoperative stoma marking is recommended for patients undergoing potential ostomy surgery. Factors influencing marking are unknown. The purpose of this study was to identify factors associated wit...

item.title: Small bowel metastasis from colorectal cancer in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a diagnostic challenge.
item.description: BMJ case reports - Case report - Small bowel metastasis from colorectal cancer (CRC) is rare and difficult to diagnose, especially in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). We report two cases with two different presentat...

item.title: Mechanistic roles of interleukin‑18 in colonic immunity across infection, inflammatory bowel disease, and colorectal cancer (Review).
item.description: International journal of molecular medicine - Review - The colon, functioning as a central organ for digestion, absorption, and immune defense in the human body, depends on intricate interactions among the epithelial barrier, immune cells, and gut micr...

item.title: From inflammation to malignancy: the dynamic evolution of cancer-associated fibroblasts in IBD-CRC.
item.description: Neoplasia (New York, N.Y.) - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease-associated colorectal cancer (IBD-CRC) represents a distinct clinical and molecular entity characterized by long-standing inflammation and a unique "dysplasia-carcinoma" ...
